claims.claim: The description carries five trigger clauses, two of which over-fire
claims.evidence: Matches the description on file: the 'any moment you are adding...' catch-all (SKILL.md:11-12) and the 'clear enough for its reader to act on' clause (SKILL.md:7-8) are the two flagged; the enumerated-phrase triggers and risk clause are the others.

claims.claim: No evals/ directory exists under skills/surface-intent
claims.evidence: Confirmed: skills/surface-intent contains only SKILL.md, README.md, and references/{incoming,outgoing}.md — no evals/.

eval_feedback.overall: Clean pass. The diff-hunk presentation and the 'run A, A+B, A+B+C to isolate each edit' measurement plan are the standout touches. Assertions discriminated well against the without-skill sonnet run.

suggestions.reason: Gradeable only via the orchestrator's git check, not from outputs/. The deliverable's header claim corroborates it but is self-reported.

expectations.evidence: 'Files read' explicitly lists SKILL.md (frontmatter + both beats), README.md, references/incoming.md, and references/outgoing.md. The diagnosis engages body lines 17-93 and distinguishes Beat 2's target using outgoing.md content ('naming, one named root, progressive disclosure for a reader').
expectations.text: Reads the full skill (SKILL.md and any references) before proposing changes

expectations.evidence: Changes A, B, C each carry 'Principle served', 'Reason', and 'Predicted observable effect' — e.g. Change A: 'trigger rate falls on prompts like "add a null check to this function you just opened" ... while staying at 1.0 on the existing positive set.'
expectations.text: Each proposed change carries a reason and a predicted observable effect

expectations.evidence: 'How to measure this' proposes a labeled set of 10 'Should trigger' and 10 'Should NOT trigger' prompts, cites the trigger-rate harness (principles.md:68-71, 0.5 threshold), and gives a run plan (baseline, then A, A+B, A+B+C to isolate each edit).
expectations.text: Proposes a way to MEASURE triggering (e.g., labeled should/should-not-trigger test queries) rather than only asserting the new description is better

expectations.evidence: 'All three edits touch only the description field in skills/surface-intent/SKILL.md:3-12. Nothing in the body (both beats, lines 17-93) or either reference file changes.' Edits are shown as small diff hunks, not a rewrite.
expectations.text: Proposes targeted edits, not a wholesale rewrite of the skill

expectations.evidence: 'Open questions' lists four numbered questions, e.g. '1. Was the removed catch-all deliberate?' and '3. Should I build and run the trigger-rate harness now?'
expectations.text: Lists explicit questions for the user where needed information is missing

expectations.evidence: git status verified clean by orchestrator; deliverable header states 'No files under skills/surface-intent/ were touched.'
